entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop (Arachis hypogaea) studied for its nitrogen fixation capabilities.

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into a usable form for plants.

entities.name: NODULATION
entities.description: The formation of root nodules where nitrogen-fixing bacteria reside.

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop used in intercropping and rotational systems with groundnut.

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop used in intercropping and rotational systems with groundnut.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop used in association with groundnut.

entities.name: PURE CULTURE
entities.description: The practice of growing a single crop species without other accompanying crops.

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops in proximity, such as groundnut with cereals.

entities.name: ROTATIONAL SYSTEMS
entities.description: The practice of growing different crops in succession on the same land.

entities.name: CEREAL
entities.description: A category of grass crops including pearl millet, maize, and sorghum.

entities.name: SHADING
entities.description: The reduction of sunlight reaching a plant due to the presence of taller neighboring plants.

entities.name: PHOTOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: The process by which green plants use sunlight to synthesize nutrients from carbon dioxide and water.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The measure of crop production per unit area.

relationships.description: reduction in photosynthesis leads to reduced nitrogen fixation
affects the yield of subsequent crops
increases the yield of following crops in rotation compared to maize
